title: numeric promotion rules
manager: The exact, spelled-out rules for when one numeric type becomes another — and, crucially, that it never happens silently across the lines that matter (int to float, bounded to unbounded). Implicit numeric promotion is a classic bug factory; FSL makes the safe widenings automatic and the lossy conversions require you to say so, so a machine can't quietly turn an exact decimal into a drifting float and only notice three states later.
cs: a total, spelled promotion lattice; widening within a kind may be implicit; int->float and bounded->unbounded are explicit-only; feeds the type checker's arithmetic result typing.
